description:
Advances: mutagen.mutation_enumeration, mutagen.mutators

Deliverable:
1. New module `lib/mutagen_ex/mutators/dispatch.ex` exposing `MutagenEx.Mutators.Dispatch.mutators_for_node/1`. The module carries a static mapping head_atom => mutator list AND a list of `:any` mutators (Literal, ResultTuple, etc. — mutators that match bare literals which are NOT 3-tuples). Per mutagen.decision.static_mutator_dispatch, this is a pure module — NO behaviour callback.
2. `mutators_for_node/1` returns a sub-sequence of `MutagenEx.Mutators.all/0`, filtered in canonical order. This is the order-preserving property required for byte-identity (mutagen.r2 determinism).
3. `MutagenEx.MutationEnumerator.try_mutators/6` consults `Dispatch.mutators_for_node/1` to pre-filter the mutator list before calling `match?/1` on each. Add a `:dispatch_mode` option to `enumerate/4` (default `:head_atom`, override `:legacy` for the equivalence test ONLY).
4. New `test/mutagen_ex/head_atom_dispatch_test.exs` with the equivalence test: for a corpus of AST nodes, assert `Mutators.all/0` (legacy path) and `Dispatch.mutators_for_node/1` (new path), applied via `match?/1` filtering, produce identical mutator lists for each node.
5. Revise `.spec/specs/mutation_enumeration.spec.md` to document the dispatch optimization (likely a new requirement covering the order-preserving + equivalence properties).
6. NO mutator behaviour change. `mutators.ex` is NOT touched.

Verification:
- Tests to pass: `mix test` green; new `head_atom_dispatch_test.exs` green (equivalence holds for the corpus); existing `test/mutagen_ex/mutation_enumerator_test.exs` green
- Behaviors to demonstrate: determinism test green (the byte-identity check would catch any reordering bug)

Out of Scope (files):
- lib/mutagen_ex/mutators/*.ex EXCEPT the new dispatch.ex (do not touch existing mutator implementations)
- lib/mutagen_ex/mutators.ex (the behaviour module — DO NOT add a `head_atoms/0` callback)
- lib/mutagen_ex/mutation_runner.ex (S4/S5 own)
- lib/mutagen_ex/ast_cache.ex (S2 owns)
- lib/mutagen_ex/beam_cache.ex (S5 owns)
Allowed touches:
- lib/mutagen_ex/mutators/dispatch.ex (new)
- lib/mutagen_ex/mutation_enumerator.ex (try_mutators/6 + :dispatch_mode opt only)
- test/mutagen_ex/head_atom_dispatch_test.exs (new)
- .spec/specs/mutation_enumeration.spec.md
- CHANGELOG.md

Out of Scope (intent):
- Do NOT add `@callback head_atoms/0` or `@optional_callbacks head_atoms: 0` to mutators.ex. This is a HARD constraint per mutagen.decision.static_mutator_dispatch. The auditor will check.
- Do NOT touch any mutator module (literal.ex, arith.ex, case_drop.ex, etc.). The dispatch table lives in one file.
- Do NOT reorder Mutators.all/0. The dispatch table FILTERS, never reorders.
- The `:dispatch_mode` option is a test seam only — it is NOT documented in the public API and is NOT exposed via the Mix task.

Merge gate:
- [ ] `mix compile --warnings-as-errors` green
- [ ] `mix test` green
- [ ] Determinism test green (catches reordering regressions)
- [ ] Head-atom equivalence test green
- [ ] Mutator behaviour callback list unchanged (grep verification: `@callback` + `@optional_callbacks` lines in mutators.ex unchanged)
- [ ] Scope check: diff touches only Allowed globs
- [ ] Auditor APPROVE comment on the ticket

Commit: `mutagen-wrd.25.03: head-atom dispatch table + enumerator pre-filter`

Determinism: full Mutators.all/0 order preserved (Dispatch.mutators_for_node/1 returns a sub-sequence of Mutators.all/0; pre_filter_mutators/3 intersects against the caller-supplied :mutators list while keeping that list's order). Locked by enumerator-level byte-identity test and by the Dispatch sub-sequence test.

Summary: Built `MutagenEx.Mutators.Dispatch` as a static map from AST head atom to mutator list plus an `:any` bucket for non-3-tuple-matching mutators (Literal for bare scalars + `__block__` wrappers; ResultTuple for tagged 2-tuples). Wired `MutationEnumerator.try_mutators/6` to consult `Dispatch.mutators_for_node/1` as an order-preserving pre-filter via a new internal `:dispatch_mode` option that defaults to `:head_atom`; the `:legacy` mode is a test-only seam. Equivalence + byte-identity properties are covered by a 47-test file that exercises both per-node mutator-set equality and full enumerator output equality across both paths. No mutator behaviour change; the `mutagen.mutators` callback surface is byte-identical to its pre-commit state.

Notes:
- Source-claim trace: Dispatch.@table at lib/mutagen_ex/mutators/dispatch.ex:67
  enumerates :+/:-/:*/:/ → Arith, :== / :!= / :< / :>= / :> / :<= → Compare,
  :and/:or/:&&/:||/:not/:! → Boolean, :with → [WithSwap, ElseRemoval],
  :if → [ElseRemoval], :case/:cond → CaseDrop, :|> → Pipeline, :when →
  GuardDrop. Cross-checked against each mutator's match?/1 head clauses
  (arith.ex:15, compare.ex:26, boolean.ex:23–25, with_swap.ex:46,
  else_removal.ex:43–48, case_drop.ex:58–63, pipeline.ex:34,
  guard_drop.ex:34–35). @any_mutators = [Literal, ResultTuple] is
  consistent with literal.ex:60–73 (bare scalars + :__block__ wrappers,
  no fixed head atom) and result_tuple.ex:29–31 (2-tuples, not 3-tuples).
  Every head atom enumerated in spec scenario s9 is covered by the table.
- Order-preservation: mutators_for_node/1 at dispatch.ex:152 filters
  Mutators.all/0, never re-orders. pre_filter_mutators/3 at
  mutation_enumerator.ex:422 intersects against the caller-supplied
  :mutators list while iterating in that list's order — preserves the
  sub-sequence property when callers pass a custom :mutators (e.g.
  arith-only in a unit test).
- The equivalence test's corpus includes "function-clause guard" and
  "anonymous function with guard clause" which generate :when 3-tuples,
  exercising the GuardDrop entry in the table. The integration corpus
  covers six multi-mutator fixtures (arith / compare+guard /
  case_drop+with / literal+pipeline / boolean+if-else / result_tuple) —
  the dispatch table touches all production paths through one or more
  of these.
- The unit-level per-node test compares `Mutators.all() |> Enum.filter(match?)`
  to `Dispatch.mutators_for_node(node) |> Enum.filter(match?)`. Strictly,
  r9's correctness clause says the *match? set* is identical — the test
  asserts list equality, which is the stronger property (list ⇒ set
  under filter-by-match?). This is fine; the integration test at :308
  separately locks byte-identity of full site lists, which proves
  Order-preservation at the enumerator boundary.

AUDIT: APPROVE
Session: a1bdcc8b01ead7600 (subagent of 1932fee7-a5d3-4675-b46d-9a3eca264d33)
Log: ~/.claude-personal/projects/-Users-mz-dev-mz-mutagen-ex/1932fee7-a5d3-4675-b46d-9a3eca264d33/subagents/agent-a1bdcc8b01ead7600.jsonl

Reasoning: r9's two contractual properties are each gated by load-bearing
assertions. (1) Equivalence is enforced by the per-node corpus test at
test/mutagen_ex/head_atom_dispatch_test.exs:145-162 — for every node in the
39-entry corpus it asserts `Mutators.all() |> Enum.filter(match?) ==
Dispatch.mutators_for_node(node) |> Enum.filter(match?)`; omitting any
mutator from a dispatch entry (e.g. dropping GuardDrop from `:when`) makes
the `:when`-containing corpus rows ("function-clause guard", "anonymous
function with guard clause", "case with guard") diverge. (2) Order-
preservation is gated twice: dispatch_test.exs:165-209 walks 10 probe shapes
and asserts the result's indices into `Mutators.all/0` are strictly
increasing — returning a permutation flips this immediately. And the
enumerator-level byte-identity at dispatch_test.exs:308-335 reruns each of
6 multi-mutator fixtures under both `:dispatch_mode` modes and asserts full
map equality plus site-id-order equality — any enumerator-level reordering
between paths bounces here. r1 stays locked under the new default via
mutation_enumerator_test.exs:48 (two-run byte-identity) and :74 (10-run)
which both run with the production `:head_atom` mode now; full enumerator
suite (30/30) green. Scope verification holds: dispatch.ex (new) +
enumeration plumbing only — `git diff b343109 HEAD -- lib/mutagen_ex/mutators.ex
lib/mutagen_ex/mutators/{arith,literal,result_tuple,case_drop}.ex` is empty
(verified, 0 lines), `@callback`/`@optional_callbacks` surface in
mutators.ex unchanged; `grep -rn dispatch_mode` confirms the option is
referenced only in mutation_enumerator.ex + head_atom_dispatch_test.exs
(no Mix task exposure). The Dispatch table covers all 10 mutators in
`Mutators.all/0` (8 head-keyed: Arith/Compare/Boolean/WithSwap/ElseRemoval/
CaseDrop/Pipeline/GuardDrop; 2 in `@any_mutators`: Literal/ResultTuple) —
spot-checked each module's match?/1 head clauses against the table entry,
all consistent (Literal correctly stays in `:any` because its
`{:__block__, _, [v]}` clauses span shapes that no head-keyed entry can
cover without aliasing). Intent-level scope clean: no @callback head_atoms/0
added, no mutator module touched, Mutators.all/0 order preserved (mutators.ex
diff empty), :dispatch_mode is documented as an internal test seam in both
the @doc and CHANGELOG, and the Mix task does not reference it.
APPROVED. Merged into epic/mutagen-wrd (fast-forward, post-merge smoke 600/0/16-excluded + format clean). Closing per orchestrator sign-off.

title: S3: head-atom dispatch table + enumerator pre-filter
